classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Then verily the first [covenant] had also ordinances of divine service, and a worldly Sanctuary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For there was a tabernacle made; the first, wherein was the Candlestick, and the Table, and the Shew-bread, which is called the Sanctuary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And after the second veil, the tabernacle which is called the Holiest of all; which had the golden censer and the Ark of the Covenant overlaid round about with gold: wherein was the golden pot that had manna, and Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant: and over it the Cherubim of glory, shadowing the Mercy-seat: of which we cannot now speak particularly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He has shown from the Priest, from the Priesthood, from the Covenant, that that [dispensation] was to have an end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: From this point he shows it from the fashion of the tabernacle itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This, he says, [was] the “Holy” and the “Holy of Holies.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The holy place then is a symbol of the former period (for there all things are done by means of sacrifices); but the Holy of Holies of this that is now present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And by the Holy of Holies he means Heaven; and by the veil, Heaven, and the Flesh “entereth into that within the veil”: that is to say, “through the veil of His flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And it were well to speak of this passage, taking it up from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then does he say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Then verily the first had also” (the first what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “The Covenant”).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Ordinances of Divine service.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What are “ordinances”? symbols or rights.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then; as (he means) it has not now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He shows that it had already given place, for (he says) it had at that time; so that now, although it stood, it is not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And the worldly Sanctuary.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He calls it “worldly,” inasmuch as it was permitted to all to tread it, and in the same house the place was manifest where the priests stood, where the Jews, the Proselytes, the Grecians, the Nazarites.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since, therefore even Gentiles were permitted to tread it, he calls it “worldly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For surely the Jews were not “the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For” (he says) “there was a tabernacle made; the first, which is called holy, wherein was the Candlestick, and the Table, and the Shew-bread.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: These things are symbols of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And after the second veil” (There was then not one veil [only], but there was a veil without also) “the tabernacle, which is called holy of holies.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Observe how everywhere he calls it a tabernacle in regard of [God’s] encamping there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Which had” (he says) “a golden Censer, and the ark of the Covenant overlaid round about with gold: wherein was the golden pot that held the manna, and Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All these things were venerable and conspicuous memorials of the Jewish obstinacy; “and the tables of the covenant” (for they brake them) “And the manna” (for they murmured; and therefore handing on the memory thereof to posterity, He commanded it to be laid up in a golden pot).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And Aaron’s rod that budded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And over it, the Cherubim of glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “the Cherubim of glory”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He either means “the glorious,” or those which are under God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Shadowing the mercy-seat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But in another point of view also he extols these things in his discourse, in order to show that those which come after them are greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Of which” (he says) “we cannot now speak particularly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In these words he hints that these were not merely what was seen, but were a sort of enigmas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Of which” (he says) “we cannot now speak particularly,” perhaps because they needed a long discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle accomplishing the service [of God].”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is, these things indeed were [there], but the Jews did not enjoy them: they saw them not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that they were no more theirs than [ours] for whom they prophesied.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 7 ) “But into the second the High Priest went alone once every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and for the errors of the people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that the types were already laid down beforehand? for, lest they should say, “how is there [but] one sacrifice?” he shows that this was so from the beginning, since at least the more holy and the awful [sacrifice] was [but] one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And how did the High Priest offer once for all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus were they wont [to do] from the beginning, for then also (he says) “the High Priest” offered “once for all.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And well said he, “not without blood.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (Not indeed without blood, yet not this blood, for the business was not so great.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He signifies that there shall be a sacrifice, not consumed by fire, but rather distinguished by blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For inasmuch as he called the Cross a sacrifice, though it had neither fire, nor logs, nor was offered many times, but had been offered in blood once for all; he shows that the ancient sacrifice also was of this kind, was offered “once for all” in blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Which he offers for himself;” again, “for himself; and for the errors of the people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said not “sins”; but “errors,” that they might not be high-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For even if thou hast not sinned intentionally, yet unintentionally thou hast erred, and from this no man is pure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And everywhere [he adds] the “for himself,” showing that Christ is much greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if He be separated from our sins, how did He “offer for Himself”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then saidst thou these things (one says)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because this is [a mark] of One that is superior.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus far there is no speculation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But from this point he philosophizes and says, ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 8 ) “The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the Holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this cause (he says) have these things been thus “ordained,” that we might learn that “the Holy of Holies,” that is, Heaven, is as yet inaccessible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not then think (he says) that because we do not enter them, they have no existence: inasmuch as neither did we enter the Most Holy [place].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Which” (he says) “was established as a figure for the time then present.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What does he mean by “the time present”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That before the coming of Christ: For after the coming of Christ, it is no longer a time present: For how [could it be], having arrived, and being ended?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There is too something else which he indicates, when he says this, “which [was] a figure for the time then present,” that is, became the Type.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “In which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service perfect, as pertaining to the conscience.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest now what is [the meaning of] “The Law made nothing perfect,” ( Heb. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 19 ,) and “If that first [covenant] had been faultless.” ( Heb. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “As pertaining to the conscience.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the sacrifices did not put away the defilement from the soul, but still were concerned with the body: “after the law of a carnal commandment.” ( Heb. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For certainly they could not put away adultery, nor murder, nor sacrilege.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou hast eaten this, Thou hast not eaten that, which are matters of indifference. [“Which stood] only in meats and drinks, and divers washings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Thou hast drunk this,” he says: and yet nothing has been ordained concerning drink, but he said this, treating them as trifles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And [in] divers washings, and carnal ordinances imposed on them until the time of reformation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this is the righteousness of the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he depreciates the sacrifices, showing that they had no efficacy, and that they existed “till the time of reformation,” that is, they waited for the time that reformeth all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But Christ being come an High Priest of good things that are come by a greater and more perfect tabernacle not made with hands.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he means the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And well did he say, “greater and more perfect,” since God The Word and all the power of The Spirit dwells therein; “For God giveth not the Spirit by measure [unto Him].” ( John iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And “more perfect,” as being both unblamable, and setting right greater things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “That is, not of this creation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: See how [it was] “greater.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it would not have been “of the Spirit” ( Matt. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 20 ), if man had constructed it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nor yet is it “of this creation”; that is, not of these created things, but spiritual, of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how he calls the body tabernacle and veil and heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By a greater and more perfect tabernacle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Through the veil, that is, His flesh.” ( Heb. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “into that within the veil.” ( Heb. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “entering into the Holy of Holies, to appear before the face of God.” ( Heb. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then doth he this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: According as one thing or a different one is signified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I mean for instance, the Heaven is a veil, for as a veil it walls off the Holy of Holies; the flesh [is a veil] hiding the Godhead; and the tabernacle likewise holding the Godhead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Again, Heaven [is] a tabernacle: for the Priest is there within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But Christ” (he says) “being come an High Priest”: he did not say, “become,” but “being come,” that is, having come for this very purpose, not having been successor to another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He did not come first and then become [High Priest], but came and became at the same time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he did not say “being come an High Priest” of things which are sacrificed, but “of good things that are come,” as if his discourse had not power to put the whole before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Neither by the blood,” he says, “of goats and calves” (All things are changed) “but by His own Blood” (he says) “He entered in once for all into the Holy Place.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: See thus he called Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Once for all” (he says) “He entered into the Holy Place, having obtained eternal redemption.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this [expression] “having obtained,” was [expressive] of things very difficult, and that are beyond expectation, how by one entering in, He “obtained everlasting redemption.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Next [comes] that which is calculated to persuade.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For if the blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the purifying of the flesh; how much more shall the Blood of Christ, who through the Holy Spirit offered Himself without spot to God, purge your conscience from dead works, to serve the living God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For (he says) if “the blood of bulls” is able to purify the flesh, much rather shall the Blood of Christ wipe away the defilement of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that thou mayest not suppose when thou hearest [the word] “sanctifieth,” that it is some great thing, he marks out and shows the difference between each of these purifyings, and how the one of them is high and the other low.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And says it is [so] with good reason, since that is “the blood of bulls,” and this “the Blood of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nor was he content with the name, but he sets forth also the manner of the offering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Who” (he says) “through the Holy Spirit offered Himself without spot to God,” that is, the victim was without blemish, pure from sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this is [the meaning of] “through the Holy Spirit,” not through fire, nor through any other things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Shall purge your conscience” (he says) “from dead works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And well said he “from dead works”; if any man touched a dead body, he was polluted; and here, if any man touch a “dead work,” he is defiled through his conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “To serve” (he says) “the Living and true God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he declares that it is not [possible] while one has “dead works to serve the Living and true God,” for they are both dead and false; and with good reason [he says this].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let no man then enter in here with “dead works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if it was not fit that one should enter in who had touched a dead body, much more one that hath “dead works”: for this is the most grievous pollution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And “dead works” are, all which have not life, which breathe forth an ill odor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as a dead body is useful to none of the senses, but is even annoying to those who come near it, so sin also at once strikes the reasoning faculty, and does not allow the understanding itself to be calm, but disturbs and troubles it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And it is said too that a plague at its very commencement corrupts the living bodies; such also is sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It differs in nothing from a plague, not [indeed] corrupting the air first, and then the bodies, but darting at once into the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou not how persons affected with the plague, are inflamed: how they writhe about, how they are full of an ill scent, how disfigured are their countenances: how wholly unclean they are?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Such are they also that sin, though they see it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, tell me, is not he who is possessed by the desire of riches or carnal lust, worse than any one that is in a fever?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is he not more unclean than all these, when he does and submits to all shameless things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what is baser than a man who is in love with money?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whatever things women that are harlots or on the stage refuse not to do neither does he [refuse].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Rather it is likely that they would refuse [to do] a thing, rather than he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He even submits to do things fit for slaves, flattering those whom he ought not; again he is overbearing where he ought not to be, being inconsistent in every respect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He will sit by flattering wicked people, and oftentimes depraved old men, that are of much poorer and meaner condition than himself; and will be insolent and overbearing to others that are good and in all respects virtuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest in both respects the baseness, the shamelessness: he is both humble beyond measure, and boastful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Harlots however stand in front of their house, and the charge against them is that they sell their body for money: yet, one may say, poverty and hunger compel them (although at the most this is no sufficient excuse: for they might gain a livelihood by work).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But the covetous man stands, not before his house, but before the midst of the city, making over to the devil not his body but his soul; so that he [the devil] is in his company, and goes in unto him, as verily to a harlot: and having satisfied all his lusts departs; and all the city sees it, not two or three persons only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this again is the peculiarity of harlots, that they are his who gives the gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Even if he be a slave or a gladiator, or any person whatever, yet if he offers their hire, they receive him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But the free, even should they be more noble than all, they do not accept without the money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: These men also do the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: They turn away right thoughts when they bring no money; but they associate with the abominable, and actually with those that fight with wild beasts, for the sake of the gold, and associate with them shamelessly and destroy the beauty of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as those women are naturally of odious appearance and black, and awkward and gross, and formless and ill-shaped, and in all respects disgusting, such do the souls of these men become, not able to conceal their deformity by their outward paintings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when the ill look is extreme, whatever they may devise, they cannot succeed in their feigning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that shamelessness makes harlots, hear the prophet saying, “Thou wert shameless towards all; thou hadst a harlot’s countenance.” ( Jer. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This may be said to the covetous also: “Thou wert shameless towards all,” not towards these or those, but “towards all.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Such an one respects neither father, nor son, nor wife, nor friend, nor brother, nor benefactor, nor absolutely any one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And why do I say friend, and brother, and father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He respects not God Himself, but all [we believe] seems to him a fable; and he laughs, intoxicated by his great lust, and not even admitting into his ears any of the things which might profit him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But O! their absurdity! and then what things they say!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Woe to thee, O Mammon, and to him that has thee not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: At this I am torn to pieces with indignation: for woe to those who say these things, though they say them in jest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For tell me, has not God uttered such a threat as this, saying, “Ye cannot serve two masters”? ( Matt. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And dost thou set at nought the threat?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Does not Paul say that it is Idolatry, and does he not call “the covetous man an Idolater”? ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And thou standest laughing, raising a laugh after the manner of women of the world who are on the stage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This has overthrown, this has cast down everything.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Our affairs, both our business and our politeness, are turned into laughing; there is nothing steady, nothing grave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I say not these things to men of the world only; but I know those whom I am hinting at.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the Church has been filled with laughter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whatever clever thing one may say, immediately there is laughter among those present: and the marvelous thing is that many do not leave off laughing even during the very time of the prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Everywhere the devil leads the dance, he has entered into all, is master of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Christ is dishonored, is thrust aside; the Church is made no account of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do ye not hear Paul saying, Let “filthiness and foolish talking and jesting” ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 4 ) be put away from you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He places “jesting” along with “filthiness,” and dost thou laugh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “foolish talking”? that which has nothing profitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And dost thou, a solitary, laugh at all and relax thy countenance? thou that art crucified? thou that art a mourner? tell me, dost thou laugh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Where dost thou hear of Christ doing this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nowhere: but that He was sad indeed oftentimes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For even when He looked on Jerusalem, He wept; and when He thought on the Traitor He was troubled; and when He was about to raise Lazarus, He wept; and dost thou laugh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If he who grieves not over the sins of others deserves to be accused, of what consideration will he be worthy, who is without sorrow for his own sins, yea laughs at them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is the season of grief and tribulation, of bruising and bringing matter [the body], of conflicts and sweatings, and dost thou laugh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost not thou see how Sarah was rebuked? dost thou not hear Christ saying, “Woe to them that laugh, for they shall weep”? ( Luke vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou chantest these things every day, for, tell me, what dost thou say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “I have laughed?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By no means; but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “I labored in my groaning.” ( Ps. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But perchance there are some persons so dissolute and silly as even during this very rebuke to laugh, because forsooth we thus discourse about laughter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For indeed such is their derangement, such their madness, that it does not feel the rebuke.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The Priest of God is Standing, offering up the prayer of all: and art thou laughing, having no fears?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And while he is offering up the prayers in trembling for thee, dost thou despise all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hearest thou not the Scripture saying, “Woe, ye despisers!” (cf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 41 from Hab. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 5 ); dost thou not shudder? dost thou not humble thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Even when thou enterest a royal palace, thou orderest thyself in dress, and look, and gait, and all other respects: and here where there is the true Palace, and things like those of heaven, dost thou laugh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou indeed, I know, seest [them] not, but hear thou that there are angels present everywhere, and in the house of God especially they stand by the King, and all is filled by those incorporeal Powers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This my discourse is addressed to women also, who in the presence of their husbands indeed do not dare readily to do this, and even if they do it, it is not at all times, but during a season of relaxation, but here they do it always.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Tell me, O woman, dost thou cover thine head and laugh, sitting in the Church?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Didst thou come in here to make confession of sins, to fall down before God, to entreat and to supplicate for the transgressions thou hast wretchedly committed, and dost thou do this with laughter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How then wilt thou be able to propitiate Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But (one says) what harm is there in laughter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There is no harm in laughter; the harm is when it is beyond measure, and out of season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Laughter has been implanted in us, that when we see our friends after a long time, we may laugh; that when we see any persons downcast and fearful, we may relieve them by our smile; not that we should burst out violently and be always laughing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Laughter has been implanted in our soul, that the soul may sometimes be refreshed, not that it may be quite relaxed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For carnal desire also is implanted in us, and yet it is not by any means necessary that because it is implanted in us, therefore we should use it, or use it immoderately: but we should hold it in subjection, and not say, Because it is implanted in us, let us use it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Serve God with tears, that thou mayest be able to wash away your sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I know that many mock us, saying, “Tears directly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore it is a time for tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I know also that they are disgusted, who say, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we die.” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Vanity of vanities, all is vanity.” ( Eccles. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is not I that say it, but he who had had the experience of all things saith thus: “I builded for me houses, I planted vineyards, I made me pools of water, [I had] men servants and women servants.” ( Eccles. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what then after all these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Vanity of vanities, all is vanity.” ( Eccles. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us mourn therefore, beloved, let us mourn in order that we may laugh indeed, that we may rejoice indeed in the time of unmixed joy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For with this joy [here] grief is altogether mingled: and never is it possible to find it pure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But that is simple and undeceiving joy: it has nothing treacherous, nor any admixture.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In that joy let us delight ourselves; that let us pursue after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And it is not possible to obtain this in any other way, than by choosing here not what is pleasant, but what is profitable, and being willing to be afflicted a little, and bearing all things with thanksgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For thus we shall be able to attain even to the Kingdom of Heaven, of which may we all be counted worthy, in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father be glory, together with the Holy Ghost, now and for ever and world without end.
